element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic used throughout all levels
Advanced traversal technique; allows vertical movement and subpixel manipulation
Platforming challenge involving timing and precision
Traversal aid for crossing large horizontal gaps
Grants access to new and hidden areas; enables level revisiting

element_introduction_order.element: Hidden map pieces in treasure chests
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional collectible; collecting all five unlocks sixth bonus stage
Pattern-based combat; unique enemy per stage
Final boss; disguises itself as Glomgold; requires pattern recognition

element_introduction_order.element: Lost treasure of McDuck (optional sixth stage)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Unlocked only after collecting all map pieces
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Secret endgame content; represents ultimate challenge

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform areas with no enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear level selection allows player-controlled pacing; individual levels build from exploration/collection (low tension) to boss encounter (high tension); speedrun times cluster around 8m57s-10m11s indicating consistent rhythm structure

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden treasure chests containing map pieces signal optional areas; cane upgrades from Gyro unlock new accessible spaces within previously completed levels
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Treasure chests with map pieces hidden in non-obvious locations; bonus sixth stage only accessible when all five map pieces collected

difficulty_curve.early_game: Niagara Falls introduces basic platforming, jumping, and cane mechanics with moderate enemy density; establishes foundational skills
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Bermuda Triangle pirate ship, Mu, Egypt, and Scotland levels introduce rope, hook, raft, and springy flower mechanics with increasing enemy variety and complexity; requires mastery of movement options
difficulty_curve.late_game: Final confrontation with D-1000 on Bermuda Triangle level demands pattern recognition and precise execution; optional sixth stage (if all map pieces collected) represents peak difficulty
notable_spikes: Boss encounter at end of each main level (1-5)
D-1000 final boss requiring shapeshifting pattern mastery
Optional sixth stage with collected McDuck treasure (maximum difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each main level (1-5) follows pattern: open area exploration with enemy encounters → hidden map piece collection → platforming challenge sequences → boss encounter at level terminus
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All five main levels (Niagara Falls, Bermuda Triangle, Mu, Egypt, Scotland)

notable_level_patterns.description: New traversal mechanics tied to environmental context (water levels use rafts, mountain areas use ropes/hooks, gaps use springy flowers)
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Pirate ship in Bermuda Triangle (rafts); Scotland level (ropes and hooks)

notable_level_patterns.description: Players can return to completed levels with upgraded cane to access previously locked areas and collect additional treasures; enables soft progression gating
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All main levels support revisiting with cane upgrades

notable_level_patterns.description: Mega Man-style freely selectable level progression; player chooses order to tackle five main stages before final boss
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage select screen presents five equitable options
